events.location: Creek Nation, Five Civilized Tribes Territory
events.description: Allotment of land in severalty to Indians prior to Congressional act of 1908
Congressional act of May 27, 1908 found Indians facing new conditions without commercial experience
Members of Five Civilized Tribes contributed financially and with their lives during World War I
Competency Commission arbitrarily removed restrictions from Indians two or three years prior to 1921
Widespread sale of lands by Indians, often for fifty percent or less of real value
Issuance of tax deeds on land due to unpaid taxes after restrictions removed and land became taxable
Sale of inherited lands from deceased allottees through probate court approval, often below value
Commissioner of Indian Affairs sent circular letter to superintendents regarding releasing Indians from supervision
Board of Indian Commissioners sent letter to Commissioner enclosing field reports
Superintendent Asbury compiled information from farmers and field matrons regarding patent-in-fee Indians

financial_transactions.description: Sale of allotted lands by Indians, often for less than fifty percent of value
Sale of inherited lands through probate court, often far below real value
Leasing of surplus land for agricultural purposes for five years, cash in advance, often at inadequate rental rates
Loans secured by purchasers on land exceeding purchase price, leading to encumbrance
Payment for goods using stock sales, Indians noted as honest in meeting obligations
Tax penalties accrued on land after restrictions removed and taxes left unpaid

correspondence.sender_title: George Vaux Jr., Chairman; Malcolm McDowell, Secretary
correspondence.subject: Releasing Indians from Government Supervision
correspondence.action_requested: Review enclosed field reports regarding patent-in-fee Indians
correspondence.outcome: Enclosed eight letters from field staff

correspondence.subject: Releasing Indians from Government Supervision
correspondence.action_requested: Provide tabular statement and views on releasing Indians from supervision

correspondence.subject: Releasing Indians from Government Supervision
correspondence.action_requested: Consider enclosed letters from farmers and matrons
correspondence.outcome: Enclosed letters from Pearce, Cope, Keough, and Petzoldt

correspondence.subject: Views on releasing Indians from supervision
correspondence.action_requested: Consider merit system for restriction removal

correspondence.subject: Removal of restrictions and winding up Indian affairs
correspondence.action_requested: Enact legislation for Secretary approval of conveyances and appraisal of inherited lands

correspondence.subject: Removal of restrictions
correspondence.action_requested: Reserve one-fourth of homestead allotments inalienable during lifetime

legislative_actions.outcome: Enacted into law, affecting Five Civilized Tribes land restrictions
Proposed legislation to require Secretary of Interior approval for conveyance of deceased allottees' lands to heirs
Proposed legislation for appraisal and advertisement of inherited lands similar to allotted lands
Proposed legislation requiring lease contracts be made under supervision of field clerk or Department officer
Proposed legislation to compel school attendance for Indian children under guardianship
